title: picris pygmaea
intro: Picris pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Thistle or Small Thistle, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family that grows in dry, stony areas and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Picris pygmaea have been reported to provide relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, and to support the immune system. Picris pygmaea can be prepared as a tea or infuse its leaves and roots in hot water to create a medicinal infusion, with some herbalists also using it as a tincture or in capsule form.
